Interface RasterImage

All Superinterfaces:
Image
All Known Implementing Classes:
AWTRasterImage
public non-sealed interface RasterImage extends Image
A raster image is an image with fixed pixel resolution (e.g. PNG, JPEG). The pixels are stored in ARGB format.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    PixelReader
    createPixelReader()
    Returns a pixel reader that can read this image's contents as pixels.
    default double
    getIntrinsicHeight()
    Returns the intrinsic height of this image.
    default double
    getIntrinsicWidth()
    Returns the intrinsic width of this image.

    Methods inherited from interface Image

    getMimeType, isReady, prepare, unload

  • Method Details

    • createPixelReader

      PixelReader createPixelReader()
      Returns a pixel reader that can read this image's contents as pixels. The image must be prepared before calling this method.
      Returns:
      an object that reads this image by pixels
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the image is not ready
      See Also:
      • Image.isReady()
      • Image.prepare()

    • getIntrinsicWidth

      default double getIntrinsicWidth()
      Returns the intrinsic width of this image. Raster images have a fixed size, so it's retrieved from the image's pixel reader.

      Note: Implementations should typically cache this value for performance.

      Specified by:
      getIntrinsicWidth in interface Image
      Returns:
      the intrinsic width of this image
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the image is not ready
      See Also:
      • Image.getIntrinsicHeight()

    • getIntrinsicHeight

      default double getIntrinsicHeight()
      Returns the intrinsic height of this image. Raster images have a fixed size, so it's retrieved from the image's pixel reader.

      Note: Implementations should typically cache this value for performance.

      Specified by:
      getIntrinsicHeight in interface Image
      Returns:
      the intrinsic height of this image
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the image is not ready
      See Also:
      • Image.getIntrinsicWidth()
